final_prompt.prompt:
Create a product shot of a top-tier Swiss tourbillon wristwatch for luxury commercial photography.

Style/medium: photorealistic macro product photography, jewelry-grade studio lighting, luxury commercial still-life in the spirit of high-end watch editorial (Patek Philippe / A. Lange & Söhne campaign aesthetic)
Composition: flat-lay, watch face-up centered in frame, macro lens with razor-thin depth of field, focal point locked precisely on the tourbillon mechanism, background rendered in smooth bokeh
Subject: tourbillon wristwatch, approximately 42mm case diameter, skeletonized open-worked dial fully exposing the mechanical movement — balance wheel, hairspring, gear train, and tourbillon cage all clearly visible; 18K rose gold case; diamond-set bezel with a single row of round brilliant diamonds; deep brown crocodile-texture leather strap
Scene/backdrop: watch resting on a dark carbon fiber textured surface, no other props
Lighting/mood: multi-angle softbox jewelry lighting scheme — even, controlled highlights across the rose gold case with no blown-out specular; secondary accent light raking across the movement to sculpt gear and bridge micro-details; cool-neutral color temperature to preserve accurate metal tones
Color palette: deep charcoal carbon fiber as base, warm rose gold as hero color, white diamond highlights as accent, deep brown leather as secondary warm tone
Materials/textures: brushed and polished rose gold surfaces with distinct finish transitions, individual diamond facets with crisp reflections, crocodile leather grain with visible pore texture, carbon fiber weave pattern on surface
Constraints: color accuracy is critical — rose gold must read as warm pink-gold, not yellow gold or copper; diamond row must show individual stones; tourbillon cage must be the sharpest element in frame
Avoid: yellow-gold color cast on rose gold, overexposed hotspots on metal, blurry movement details, artificial lens flare, watermark, generic watch-ad gradient background
